title: Table Management on Informix
text: Table Management on Informix Table creation happens on the fly. Alternatively, you can use the Create Table utility to generate SQL scripts that creates tables with default row locking. Table Names Uniface allows letters, numbers, and underscores as table names. The first character must be a letter. Informix allows users to access tables belonging to other users, and in other databases. This is achieved by specifying the table name as follows: { DatabaseEnv :}{ OwnerName .} TableName Copy If you want to use the INF connector to access a table in a different database, you should specify a Uniface path to the directory. Then, when generating SQL statements, the connector prefixes TableName with the DatabaseEnv value given in that path. To access a table with a different owner, you have to prefix TableName with OwnerName in the assignment file. TableName , including the owner prefix, is always converted to lower case by the connector. Informix always interprets OwnerName as being in upper case, unless it is enclosed in double quotation marks ("). Informix user names must therefore be specified in either upper case or lower case, but never in mixed case. Overflow Tables Primary key fields are reproduced in a table of the same name with the prefix O . An overflow table is created when the table contains one of the following Uniface data types: C* VC* VU* S* VS* R* VR* Overflow tables cannot be created on the fly if referential integrity is handled by Informix. This is because Uniface uses the DBMS referential integrity controls, which cannot be created on the fly. Therefore, both the Uniface Create Table facility and the Create Script facility must be used to create tables with corresponding overflow tables and referential integrity controls. Related Links Migrating Informix Overflow Tables In all Informix connectors prior to Uniface V8.2.03-B306, the size of the base table part of the variable-length field is either 2, 4, 6, or 8 KB (depending on the sum of the size of the fixed-length fields), minus the sum of the size of the fixed-length fields. As of Uniface V8.2.03-B306, the size of the base table part of the variable-length field is always 8 KB minus the sum of the size of the fixed-length fields. This results in an incompatibility.
